name: Roast Pork Shoulder
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the picnic shoulder on a rack in a shallow roasting pan.
If using a meat thermometer, insert it into the center of the roast so that the tip does not touch the bone or fat.
2. Roast uncovered at 325°F (slow oven) until done, about 2-1/2 to 3 hours. To test for doneness, make a small cut next to the bone into the thicker part of the meat. The juices will be clear when the meat is done. If using a thermometer, it should read 170°F.
3. Remove the fat from the drippings. The defatted drippings will measure about 2 tablespoons; save half for Roast Pork with Gravy and half for Pork and Cabbage soup.
4. Separate the meat from the rind, fat, and bone. Using the smaller pieces of pork, dice and save 1 cup for Pork and Cabbage Soup. Slice the remaining meat. Save half for sandwiches and half for Roast Pork with Gravy.
5. Cooked pork will keep 3 to 4 days in the refrigerator.
